name: 2023-06-stader
note: Several findings name multiple instances of the same bug across sibling contracts; the manifest pins the primary instance's contract/file/line. M-03's function is markValidatorReadyToDeposit (the report's markValidatorStatusAsReadyToDeposit is a paraphrase).

known_findings.summary: initialise() has no access control (only an isInitialized flag); an attacker can initialise a fresh proxy and, because the proxy delegatecalls the vault implementation, drive a selfdestruct.
updateAdmin revokes DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE then grants it; setting the same address loses admin access (protocol-specific role lifecycle).
Several Pausable contracts (SocializingPool, StaderOracle, OperatorRewardsCollector, Auction) never expose pause()/unpause(), so pausing is impossible.
A single Stader OPERATOR unilaterally validates validators (markValidatorReadyToDeposit) with no appeal path — a centralization / single-point-of-failure on a privileged role.
updatePoolAddress always reverts for an existing poolId due to mutually exclusive validation conditions.
Consensus uses strict-equal submission counting vs a threshold that can never be met after trusted nodes are removed.
slashValidatorSD slashes only poolThreshold.minThreshold rather than the actual penalty, under-slashing larger penalties.
When Auction is paused, addBid is blocked but the lot still closes, letting a last-minute MEV bidder win all lots.
SD price median is built from submissions across different reporting blocks, corrupting the aggregated value (protocol-specific oracle aggregation logic).
poolIdArrayIndexForExcessDeposit / lastExcessETHDepositBlock advance even when balance is insufficient, letting deposits be mis-routed.
initialise sets owner = staderConfig.getAdmin(); if the admin mapping is unset, owner becomes address(0) (protocol-specific init dependency, distinct from the modeled missing-zero-check sink).

distributeRewards has only a partial guard (a non-operator may call it whenever totalRewards <= rewardsThreshold); calling it before settleFunds manipulates the vault balance to push a validator below threshold and make it slashable (M-11). A front-runnable griefing bug, not a clean missing-access-control sink.
settleFunds ignores the NodeELRewardVault balance when computing penalty coverage, over-charging the operator.
Fixed endBlock with no extension gives no incentive to bid early; only last-block MEV bids.
getPORFeedData uses latestRoundData() and discards updatedAt/answeredInRound, accepting stale/incorrect Proof-of-Reserve data.
